segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: Come in. It's nice to finally meet you in person.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Thank you, Professor. I'm glad to be here. I've been looking forward to this.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: How was the move to Boston?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: A little overwhelming, but manageable. I'm still finding my rhythm.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: That's completely normal. Tell me a bit about your research interests.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I'm interested in housing systems, urban everyday life, and how architecture can respond to long-term social change.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: That's a broad area. What questions are you carrying into the program?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I'm trying to understand how design decisions scale - from a room, to a building, to a neighborhood.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: Good. We'll help you sharpen that.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I appreciate that. Also, my spoken English is still catching up, so I may ask for clarification sometimes.

segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: Please do. Clarity matters more than speed.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Thank you. That helps me feel more comfortable starting out.
